title: pinus merkusii var. tonkinensis
intro: Pinus merkusii var. tonkinensis, also known as Tonkin pine or Vietnamese white pine, is a variety of pine tree native to Vietnam. It is used in traditional medicine due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of terpenes and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Pinus merkusii var. tonkinensis have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and aiding in the treatment of gastrointestinal problems. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and needles are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
